notes: Kinematic mapping v4 fixes Y table workholding movement based on NX MCD Physics Navigator.
In NX MCD, BASE_Y rigid body owns JIG, Y_SLIDER and BASE_Y collision bodies; therefore the frontend Y virtual link must move the table plus fixture/workholding objects.
The exported GLB contains GÁ PHÔI as a parent node with child nodes JIG and JIG_1; some exports may not preserve Y_SLIDER, so the map binds both parent and child names for robustness.
Z_BALL_SCREW is mounted on the X carriage: it follows X motion but does not follow Z translation.
BASE_Z is a child of the virtual X link and then moves on Z, so the spindle/head follows X and Z correctly.
Calibrate sign/model_axis using X+10, Y+10, Z+10 in the viewer.
Y_SLIDER is optional because the current GLB export does not include a node with that exact name; BASE_Y/GÁ PHÔI/JIG still bind the table/workholding motion.

X+10mm: BASE_Z/spindle/endmill also follows X through the virtual parent link
BASE_Y and GÁ PHÔI remain still
Y+10mm: GÁ PHÔI, JIG/JIG_1 and the workholding fixture follow Y
Z+10mm: Z_BALL_SCREW remains at the X-carriage height/position and only follows X, not Z
